hero_datapoints.label: What coordination can add on a $1,000,000 portfolio
hero_datapoints.basis: Vanguard Advisor's Alpha estimates best-practice wealth management adds up to, or greater than, 3.0% in net annual return versus the average self-directed investor. The ~$30,000 is the 3% translated onto a $1M balance, shown as a labeled illustration, not a promise.
hero_datapoints.evidence_level: Cited-public (the 3% is a named Vanguard study figure); the $30,000 dollar translation is Illustrative-model
hero_datapoints.source: Vanguard, 'Putting a Value on Your Value: Quantifying Vanguard's Advisor's Alpha' (2022), reaffirmed in Vanguard 'Market volatility is inevitable, Advisor's Alpha is enduring,' Mar 31 2025. Verified Jul 2026.

hero_datapoints.label: Shielded per person / per married couple from federal estate tax in 2026, now permanent
hero_datapoints.basis: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (P.L. 119-21), signed Jul 4 2025, set the federal estate and gift exemption at $15M per individual and $30M per couple effective Jan 1 2026, permanent and inflation-indexed from a 2025 base. Statutory fact.
hero_datapoints.source: OBBBA P.L. 119-21 amending IRC 2010(c)(3); IRS 2026 inflation-adjustment release (Oct 2025); Morgan Lewis, Oct 2025. Verified Jul 2026.

hero_datapoints.label: Illinois state estate tax the identical Texas family would never owe
hero_datapoints.basis: Modeled composite on a roughly $5.5M estate: Illinois taxes the estate above its $4M exclusion (first dollars near a 29% effective rate, no portable second spousal exemption) while a Texas-domiciled estate owes $0. Not an actual client outcome.
hero_datapoints.source: Modeled by Driftwood on Illinois Attorney General Form 700 computation vs the Texas $0 result; underlying thresholds 35 ILCS 405 and Texas Const. Art. 8. Verified Jul 2026.

hero_datapoints.label: The cost of missing the market's 30 best days over a decade
hero_datapoints.basis: $100,000 in the S&P 500 held every day over the 10 years ended Dec 31 2025 grew to $340,519 (241%); missing only the 30 best days left $104,013 (4%). Russell labels this for illustration only, not the return of any actual investment.
hero_datapoints.source: Russell Investments, 'Value of an Advisor: More Than a Guide,' 2026 13th Edition (Source: Morningstar, S&P 500 Index), 10 yrs ended Dec 31 2025. Verified Jul 2026.

hero_datapoints.label: What one dollar over $212,000 of joint income costs a Medicare couple
hero_datapoints.basis: IRMAA is a cliff, not a phase-in. In 2025 a couple at $212,001 MAGI is bumped a full tier: +$74.00/mo Part B and +$13.70/mo Part D per spouse, $1,052.40 each, $2,104.80 for a couple, triggered by a return filed two years earlier.
hero_datapoints.evidence_level: Cited-public (arithmetic on the published CMS schedule; 'up to,' assumes both spouses enrolled)
hero_datapoints.source: CMS 2025 Medicare Parts B & D IRMAA schedule (announced Nov 2024), via Kiplinger and ValuePenguin. Verified Jul 2026.

margin_facts.fact: Left to their own timing, investors earned 7.0% a year while their own funds returned 8.2%, a 1.2-point behavior gap over the decade ended Dec 31 2024, roughly 15% of everything the funds earned.
margin_facts.source: Morningstar, 'Mind the Gap 2025' (US), Jeffrey Ptak. Verified Jul 2026.
margin_facts.placement: Margin note beside the behavioral-coaching stat, as a 7.0% vs 8.2% two-number contrast.

margin_facts.fact: $19,000 per recipient moves out of the estate tax-free every year in 2025 and 2026, with no cap on recipients; a gift-splitting couple can move $38,000 each.
margin_facts.source: IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40 and IRS 2026 inflation-adjustment release. Verified Jul 2026.
margin_facts.placement: Recurring-lever chip in the coordinated-gifting subsection of the Opportunity Register.

margin_facts.fact: Illinois taxes an estate above a $4,000,000 exclusion (unchanged since 2012), the exclusion is NOT portable between spouses, and the first dollars above it carry a roughly 29% effective marginal rate (statutory rates to 16%).
margin_facts.source: 35 ILCS 405; Illinois Attorney General Form 700; SmartAsset and Valur, 2025. Verified Jul 2026.
margin_facts.placement: Two-line stat band anchoring the state-contrast panel, with 'no second exemption for couples' as the margin note.

margin_facts.fact: A Texas-domiciled decedent owes $0 state income tax and $0 state estate tax; 2025 Proposition 8 (approved Nov 4 2025, carrying 253 of 254 counties) permanently bars any state death or estate tax by constitutional amendment.
margin_facts.source: Texas Const. Art. 8 Sec. 24-a (2019, income tax); Texas 2025 Proposition 8 (HJR 2), approved Nov 4 2025; Ballotpedia. Verified Jul 2026.
margin_facts.placement: The $0 / $0 right-hand column opposite the Illinois figures; footnote that Prop 8 makes it permanent.

margin_facts.fact: Portability can preserve up to $30,000,000 of combined 2026 exemption for a surviving spouse, but only if a timely Form 706 electing portability is filed at the first death, even when no tax is owed.
margin_facts.placement: Missed-filing failure-mode margin note in The Standing Views / Decision Register.

margin_facts.fact: A peer-reviewed study simulating tax-loss harvesting on the 500 largest US stocks from 1926 to 2018 found an average 1.08% a year of tax alpha before costs, 0.82% after the wash-sale rule, and it applies only to taxable holdings.
margin_facts.source: Chaudhuri, Burnham & Lo, 'An Empirical Evaluation of Tax-Loss-Harvesting Alpha,' Financial Analysts Journal, Q3 2020. Verified Jul 2026.
margin_facts.placement: Academic counterweight chip beside the illustrative tax exhibit; note it scales by taxable-asset share.

margin_facts.fact: In a low-income year a married couple can realize up to $96,700 of long-term capital gains at a 0% federal rate ($48,350 single) in 2025, resetting cost basis at no federal tax.
margin_facts.source: IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40, 2025 adjustments. Verified Jul 2026.
margin_facts.placement: 'The window most people never open' callout with a small retirement-gap timeline.

margin_facts.fact: State income tax spread on income alone: Texas 0%, Illinois 4.95% flat, California 13.3% top marginal (12.3% bracket plus a 1% surtax over $1,000,000), the highest in the country.
margin_facts.source: Texas Const. Art. 8; Illinois 35 ILCS 5; California FTB 2025 schedules; Tax Foundation 2025. Verified Jul 2026.
margin_facts.placement: Small three-rung domicile ladder (TX / IL / CA) separate from the estate-tax point.

harris_illustrative.item: Illinois vs Texas state estate tax (the flagship finding)
harris_illustrative.note: Hypothetical, for illustration. Modeled on a roughly $5.5M Harris estate: Illinois tax above the $4M exclusion with no portable second exemption vs the Texas $0 constitutional result. A domicile-and-titling outcome, not investment performance, not an actual client result.

harris_illustrative.item: Hidden fund tax drag on the taxable equity sleeve
harris_illustrative.note: Hypothetical, for illustration. Applies Morningstar's 1.90% mutual-fund vs 0.70% ETF tax-cost ratios to a $2,000,000 taxable balance (~$38,000 vs ~$14,000). A vehicle-and-location outcome; the published ratios are cited third-party research, the dollar figure is modeled.

Hypothetical, for illustration (Russell 2026). Traditional investor lost $20,944 (2.1%) to federal tax on the same $1M; tax-aware lost $833 (0.1%). Russell labels this illustrative, not any actual strategy; tax outcomes vary.

harris_illustrative.item: IRMAA cliff exposure for the Harris couple
harris_illustrative.figure: $2,105 a year at the first tier; up to $6,356 per spouse at the top
harris_illustrative.note: Composite illustration on the published 2025 CMS schedule. Both spouses assumed enrolled; the trigger is MAGI from a return filed two years earlier, exactly where an uncoordinated Roth conversion or RMD lands them over $212,000.

harris_illustrative.item: 0% capital-gains harvesting window in the pre-Social-Security gap
harris_illustrative.figure: up to $96,700 of gains at 0% federal
harris_illustrative.note: Illustrative application of the 2025 MFJ 0% LTCG ceiling to the Harris low-income years. Depends on total taxable income; not tax advice.
Cited IRS threshold ($19,000 each, gift-splitting) applied to the Harris family. Any multi-year cumulative total shown on the page is Illustrative-model, not a promise.
Cited statutory ceiling (OBBBA) as it applies to the Harris couple, and contingent on a Form 706 portability election at the first death. Framed as current law subject to future legislation.

Composite illustration on the 2025 IRMAA schedule: at the first spouse's death the couple's thresholds roughly halve on single filing, so income that was safe now triggers surcharges. SSA-44 is the actionable reset.

coordination_failures: One dollar over $212,000 of joint income bumps a Medicare couple a full IRMAA tier, $2,105 a year, triggered by a tax return they filed two years earlier.
Miss the Form 706 filing at the first spouse's death and up to $15,000,000 of that spouse's estate exemption is gone for good; portability is never automatic.
The average stock mutual fund quietly surrenders 1.90% a year to taxes versus 0.70% for ETFs; on a $2,000,000 taxable sleeve that gap is about $24,000 the statement never shows.
Left to their own timing, investors gave up 1.2 percentage points a year, roughly 15% of everything their own funds earned.
In 2024, more than 80% of US stock mutual funds handed shareholders a taxable gain they never asked for, versus about 5% of equity ETFs.
When one spouse dies the IRMAA line drops from $212,000 to $106,000, so the same retirement income that was safe as a couple now triggers surcharges for the survivor.
Die an Illinois resident with an estate over $4,000,000 and the first dollars carry a roughly 29% effective rate with no second spousal exemption; the identical estate in Texas owes $0.
Cross $500,000 of MAGI and the new $40,000 SALT deduction phases down toward its $10,000 floor by $600,000, a torpedo band where each added dollar is taxed punishingly.
Panic out of the market and miss only its 30 best days over a decade and $340,519 becomes $104,013, a $236,506 gap from days no one can predict.

layout_treatments.treatment: A newspaper-style engraved nameplate across the full bleed, with a single hero stat band beneath it carrying only the ~3% / ~$30,000 coordination anchor and a one-line thesis. No body copy above the fold.
layout_treatments.why_not_ledger: A masthead sets institutional authority and commits the page to one thesis number; a table or list here would bury the lede and read as data before the reader trusts the source.

layout_treatments.treatment: A single framed dossier card, a household 'file' with a silhouette monogram, a short standfirst introducing the composite once, and three or four key facts as labeled tabs rather than rows. A visible 'Composite household, illustrative' stamp on the card.
layout_treatments.why_not_ledger: The Harris family must land as a person introduced once, not a data record; a ledger depersonalizes them and invites the reader to treat the composite as an actual client.

layout_treatments.treatment: A vertical section index rendered as a masthead contents spine, each view a linked chip with a short gloss, like a newspaper's front-page index down the left rail.
layout_treatments.why_not_ledger: This is navigation, not evidence; it should feel like an editorial index that orients the reader, and numbers in rows here would compete with the actual findings downstream.

layout_treatments.treatment: An annotated stack of finding cards, each an index-card tile with one large dollar figure, a one-line mechanic, a source footnote, and its own evidence-level and 'illustrative' tag where needed. Cards fan or grid, they do not align into a spreadsheet.
layout_treatments.why_not_ledger: The value lives in each distinct, self-contained opportunity; cards let every finding breathe and carry its own label, whereas ledger rows flatten a $395,000 insight to the visual weight of a line item and make blanket illustrative labeling impossible.

layout_treatments.treatment: A two-part spread: the Constitution article set as an engraved charter or preamble block in a serif display face, paired with the Decision Register as a vertical dated timeline of nodes (each a decision, its date, and the threshold it turned on).
layout_treatments.why_not_ledger: Decisions accrue over time and have cause and sequence; a timeline shows why one decision followed another, while a table strips the chronology and the reasoning that make the register a 'standing view' rather than a log.

layout_treatments.section: 6 The Annual Wealth Operating Review (Illinois vs Texas, $395k)
layout_treatments.treatment: A side-by-side diptych: an Illinois panel and a Texas panel facing each other, $395,000 opposite $0 at matched scale, with an annotated computation callout (the $4M exclusion, the ~29% first-dollar rate, no portability) and a prominent 'Hypothetical, for illustration, roughly $5.5M estate' stamp near the number.
layout_treatments.why_not_ledger: A two-panel contrast makes the gap visceral and shows it is a domicile-and-titling choice, not investment performance; a single number in a row would neither dramatize the spread nor make the required hypothetical framing unmissable.

layout_treatments.treatment: A colophon-style sources footer in small caps, listing each figure with its source and as-of date and separating cited-public facts from illustrative-model figures, leading to one clear call-to-action door: a single button, no competing links.
layout_treatments.why_not_ledger: Provenance is editorial trust-building meant to be read as a citation block, and the close must be one decisive door; a menu of options or a data table would dilute both the credibility and the single next step.

compliance_summary:
Cited-public facts (safe to state as fact with source and as-of date): the 2026 $15M/$30M estate exemption and 2025 $13.99M level (OBBBA, IRS); $19,000/$38,000 annual gift exclusion; Illinois $4M non-portable exclusion and ~29% effective rate; Texas $0/$0 with 2025 Proposition 8 (NOTE: the cluster mislabeled this Prop 9; the death-tax ban is Proposition 8, approved Nov 4 2025); California 13.3% top rate; SALT $40,000 cap and $500k-$600k phase-out; QSBS $15M and 3/4/5-year tiers; IRC 1014 step-up and IRC 2010(c) portability/Form 706; all 2025 IRMAA thresholds and surcharges; 0% LTCG $96,700 ceiling; 22%/24% bracket ceilings; SSA-44. Named third-party research citable if attributed and not restated as Driftwood's own results: Vanguard Advisor's Alpha 3% and the coaching-to-2% module ranking; Morningstar Mind the Gap 1.2% behavior gap, 1.90%/0.70% tax-cost ratios, and 80%/5% distribution split; Morningstar-Blanchett Gamma +29% income; the FAJ 1.08%/0.82% tax-loss-harvesting study.

MUST be labeled 'Hypothetical, for illustration' and NOT presented as actual client results or the firm's track record (SEC Marketing Rule 206(4)-1): the $395,000 vs $0 Harris finding (disclose the ~$5.5M assumed estate); the Russell $340,519 vs $104,013 / $236,506 best-days example and the $833 vs $20,944 tax-aware example (both carry Russell's own illustrative and past-performance disclaimers, cite the index and period); the ~$24,000/yr fund tax-drag figure on $2M; the ~$30,000/yr translation of Vanguard's 3%; and every register row in the Harris household, plus any multi-year cumulative gifting total or QSBS-stacking figure.

Needs principal sign-off before publishing: all four items above, plus the Vanguard 3% and coaching stats, the Morningstar Gamma and tax-cost figures, and the FAJ study (confirm exact as-of at build). As a fee-only RIA Driftwood MAY cite this third-party research and quantify tax, gift, IRMAA, and estate mechanics as labeled illustrations. It may NOT claim investment outperformance, imply guaranteed results, present any 3% or Gamma or best-days figure as promised or as Driftwood-generated, or present any hypothetical as an actual client outcome. Frame behavioral coaching as mistake-avoidance (the 0-to-2% range can be zero in a given year), the 3% as potential value-add not a promise, and every state and estate result as current law subject to future legislation with suitability that is client-specific.
